title: Dom-Enum Open Problem
statement_text: Can the minimal dominating sets of a graph be enumerated in output-polynomial time?
context_text: It is a long-standing open problem whether the minimal dominating sets of a graph can be enumerated in output-polynomial time. The problem is equivalent to Trans-Enum (enumerating minimal transversals of a hypergraph), for which the best known algorithm runs in quasi-polynomial time due to Fredman and Khachiyan.
paper_contribution: Provides output-polynomial time and polynomial space algorithms for $(K_t+K_2)$-free graphs (and thus $K_t$-free graphs), answering an explicit question of Kanté et al. for bipartite graphs, and also for paw-free and diamond-free graphs.
notes: No specific original proposer is cited for Dom-Enum; described throughout the literature as a long-standing open problem. The Trans-Enum equivalence and Fredman–Khachiyan quasi-polynomial bound [FK96] are cited as context. Full text was truncated after Section 3; Sections 7–8 ('obstacles to stronger theorems' and 'future research directions') were not available, so any conjectures stated there could not be extracted.
paper_title: Enumerating minimal dominating sets in $K_t$-free graphs and variants
